# Financial Deep Research: Newmont Corporation (NEM)

Research conducted: 2026-05-25
Stage 1 baseline provided: No — §9 will note the absence

## 1. Recent Financial Performance & Quarterly Trends

### Most Recent Report
Newmont reported first-quarter 2026 results on April 23, 2026. Revenue totaled $7.31 billion, up 46% year-over-year from $5.01 billion in Q1 2025. Reported net income was $3.3 billion, and adjusted net income (ANI) was $3.2 billion ($2.90 per diluted share). Adjusted EBITDA reached $5.2 billion. The company generated $3.8 billion in cash from operating activities and a record quarterly free cash flow (FCF) of $3.1 billion. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] ***

Gold production was 1.30 million attributable ounces, down 10% sequentially from Q4 2025, with silver production of 9 million ounces and copper production of 30 thousand tonnes. Consolidated gold sales were 1.23 million ounces. Gold by-product all-in sustaining costs (AISC) were $1,029 per ounce, while gold co-product CAS was $1,307 per ounce and gold by-product CAS was $541 per ounce—an exceptionally low by-product CAS driven by favorable copper and silver volumes and pricing, as well as lower direct costs at Tanami, Lihir, and Peñasquito. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] ***

### Most Recent Full Year Report
Full-year 2025 results were reported on February 19, 2026. Attributable gold production was approximately 5.9 million ounces (consolidated ~6.0 million ounces), down 14% year-over-year, with 28 million ounces of silver and 135 thousand tonnes of copper. Gold by-product AISC was $1,358 per ounce and co-product AISC was $1,609 per ounce. Reported net income from continuing operations was $7.085 billion ($6.39 per diluted share). Adjusted net income was $7.634 billion ($6.89 per diluted share) and adjusted EBITDA was $13.48 billion, up 55% from the prior year. Net cash provided by operating activities of continuing operations was $10.334 billion, and FCF was an all-time annual record of $7.299 billion. [Source: Company FY2025 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-19] *** [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

No explicit full-year 2025 consolidated sales total was extracted from the primary filings reviewed; the 10-K narrative notes a year-over-year increase driven by higher average realized gold prices partially offset by divestitures. [Source: Company FY2025 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

### Key Business Drivers in Recent Quarters
The dominant financial driver since 2024 has been the escalation in realized gold prices. The average realized gold price was $4,900 per ounce in Q1 2026, versus $2,944 in Q1 2025 and a full-year 2025 average of $3,498. [Source: Reuters / Title: Newmont beats Q1 profit estimates as record gold prices offset... / Date: 2026-04-23] * [Source: LeverageShares / Title: Newmont Stock: Surging Gold Prices Drive Record Cash Flows / Date: 2026-02-20] * This price environment has overwhelmed volume declines and cost inflation, producing record EBITDA and FCF.

Volume trends have been negative due to the completed non-core divestiture program (CC&V, Musselwhite, Éléonore, Akyem, Porcupine, and Coffee), which removed approximately 8.6 million reserve ounces and reduced production. Q1 2026 production was also impacted by a bushfire at Boddington, record rainfall at Tanami, and planned maintenance at Lihir and Cerro Negro. Partial offsets came from Yanacocha, Cadia, Merian, and the ramp-up of Ahafo North. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] *** [Source: Company FY2025 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

### Segment Performance
Newmont’s portfolio spans Africa, Australia, Latin America & the Caribbean, North America (Nevada), and Papua New Guinea. Key managed operations include:
- **Africa (Ahafo South / Ahafo North):** Ahafo North achieved commercial production on October 24, 2025, and contributed to lower-cost production growth. FY2025 Ahafo South co-product AISC was $1,494/oz; Ahafo North was $696/oz. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Australia (Boddington, Tanami, Cadia, Lihir):** Boddington faced a bushfire in Q1 2026. Tanami experienced record rainfall. Cadia saw higher grades and throughput in Q1 2026. Lihir is advancing the Nearshore Barrier project to extend mine life beyond 2040. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] ***
- **Latin America (Peñasquito, Cerro Negro, Yanacocha):** Peñasquito delivered strong co-product silver, lead, and zinc volumes in Q1 2026. Yanacocha is focused on leach production; the sulfides project was indefinitely deferred in Q4 2025. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] *** [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Non-managed:** Nevada Gold Mines (38.5% interest, proportionately consolidated) and Pueblo Viejo (40% equity method) contributed attributable production, though Q1 2026 deliveries were lower quarter-over-quarter. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] ***

### Geographic & FX Exposure
Newmont’s 2026 guidance assumes foreign exchange rates of AUD/USD 0.70, CAD/USD 0.75, and USD/MXN 17.00. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] *** A weaker Australian and Canadian dollar relative to actual spot rates would typically benefit cost profiles, while a weaker Mexican peso would reduce Peñasquito and Cerro Negro local-currency costs. The company is also exposed to country-specific fiscal and political risk, notably in Peru (Yanacocha), Papua New Guinea (Lihir), and Ghana (Ahafo).

## 2. Growth Trajectory

### Trajectory Assessment
A two-year look-back (2024–2025) is appropriate given the step-change in commodity prices and the portfolio restructuring via divestitures. The trajectory is characterized by surging profitability on flat-to-lower volumes.

- **FY2024:** Sales of $18.682 billion; net income from continuing operations attributable to stockholders of $3.280 billion; adjusted net income of $3.991 billion; adjusted EBITDA of $8.675 billion; operating cash flow of approximately $6.36 billion; FCF of approximately $2.96 billion. [Source: Company 2024 Annual Report / Title: FY2024 Annual Report / Date: 2025-02] *** [Source: Monexa.ai / Title: Newmont Corporation (NEM): Cash Generation, Rapid Margin Recovery / Date: 2025-08-27] **
- **FY2025:** Adjusted EBITDA surged 55% to $13.48 billion; ANI rose 91% to $7.634 billion; operating cash flow jumped 64% to $10.334 billion; FCF rose approximately 150% to $7.299 billion. [Source: Company FY2025 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-19] *** [Source: LeverageShares / Title: Newmont Stock: Surging Gold Prices Drive Record Cash Flows / Date: 2026-02-20] *

However, attributable gold production declined 14% in 2025 and is guided to decline further to approximately 5.3 million ounces in 2026 (from 5.9 million in 2025). [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] *** This is a volume-contraction phase masked by extreme pricing. Management’s longer-term outlook targets approximately 6.0 million ounces of gold and 150 thousand tonnes of copper annually, contingent on project execution (Tanami Expansion 2, Cadia panel caves, Boddington stripping completion, Lihir Nearshore Barrier). [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

### Peer-Relative Growth Context
Newmont’s production trajectory diverges from some peers. Barrick Gold’s attributable production fell roughly 17% in 2025 to ~3.26 million ounces and is guided to 2.9–3.25 million ounces in 2026, with 2026 AISC guided at $1,760–$1,950 per ounce. [Source: TradingView / Title: Barrick Mining vs. Newmont: Which Gold Giant Is Shining Brighter? / Date: 2026-04-20] * Newmont’s guided 2026 AISC of $1,680/oz sits below Barrick’s range, suggesting relative cost competitiveness despite volume headwinds.

## 3. Margin & Profitability Profile

For a gold mining enterprise, the most meaningful margin metrics are not standard gross/operating/net margins alone, but rather the spread between realized metal prices and all-in sustaining costs (AISC), supplemented by co-product credits. I use AISC, CAS per ounce, and adjusted EBITDA margin because these reflect the true unit economics of extracting and replacing reserves.

### Unit Margin Expansion
- **FY2024:** Gold by-product AISC was approximately $1,516 per ounce, against an average realized gold price of $2,408/oz. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: Newmont's Soaring Unit Costs Warrant Caution / Date: 2025-06-12] * [Source: LeverageShares / Title: Newmont Stock: Surging Gold Prices Drive Record Cash Flows / Date: 2026-02-20] *
- **FY2025:** Gold by-product AISC was $1,358/oz, with an average realized price of $3,498/oz, implying a by-product margin of ~$2,140/oz before corporate costs and taxes. Co-product AISC was $1,609/oz. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Q1 2026:** Gold by-product AISC plunged to $1,029/oz, while the realized gold price hit $4,900/oz, producing an exceptional by-product margin of ~$3,871/oz. This was driven by record co-product silver and copper pricing and volumes, which inflated by-product credits. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] ***

### Profitability Quality
Adjusted EBITDA margins expanded dramatically: FY2024 adjusted EBITDA of $8.675 billion on $18.682 billion sales implies a ~46% margin; FY2025 adjusted EBITDA of $13.48 billion suggests a margin above 55% (pending exact sales total). Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA of $5.2 billion on $7.31 billion revenue yields a 71% margin, highlighting extreme operating leverage at current gold and co-product prices.

### Peer-Relative Profitability Context
Newmont’s guided 2026 AISC of $1,680/oz compares favorably to Barrick’s $1,760–$1,950/oz range and is above Agnico Eagle’s guided 2025 AISC of $1,250–$1,300/oz (though Agnico is a smaller, primarily Canadian operator with a different cost base). [Source: TradingView / Title: Barrick Mining vs. Newmont: Which Gold Giant Is Shining Brighter? / Date: 2026-04-20] * [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: Newmont's Soaring Unit Costs Warrant Caution / Date: 2025-06-12] *

## 4. Cash Generation & Quality

### Cash Generation Quality Assessment
Newmont generated $10.334 billion in operating cash flow and $7.299 billion in FCF in FY2025, rising to $3.8 billion and $3.1 billion respectively in Q1 2026. [Source: Company FY2025 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-19] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] *** For a capital-intensive mining business, FCF is the operative lens; however, current FCF must be viewed as super-cycle performance rather than through-cycle normalized generation.

Cash conversion is strong. In Q1 2026, operating cash flow of $3.8 billion compared to reported net income of $3.3 billion indicates high-quality earnings with modest working capital drag. For FY2025, operating cash flow of $10.334 billion compared to net income of $7.085 billion also signals robust conversion. [Source: Company FY2025 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

### Working Capital & Cash Conversion
Working capital has created modest quarterly volatility. In Q3 2025, unfavorable working capital movements of $286 million reduced operating cash flow, driven by a $369 million increase in receivables, $247 million of reclamation cash spend (primarily Yanacocha water treatment plants), and a $106 million inventory build. [Source: Company Q3 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Third Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2025-10-23] *** In Q1 2026, working capital impacts were $202 million. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] *** These movements are material but not structurally concerning; they reflect timing of metal sales collections and environmental remediation obligations.

### Earnings Quality
FY2025 reported net income included a net gain on completed divestments, impairment charges of $779 million (Q4) primarily related to the indefinite deferral of Yanacocha Sulfides, and tax adjustments for undistributed foreign earnings. Adjusted net income excludes these items and is the better metric for operational performance. The large gap between reported and adjusted figures in Q4 2025 ($1.30 billion reported vs. $2.75 billion adjusted) suggests reported earnings are noisy, but the adjustments are largely non-cash or one-off in nature. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

## 5. Balance Sheet & Liquidity

As of March 31, 2026, Newmont held $8.8 billion in cash and $12.8 billion in total liquidity, with a net cash position of $3.2 billion. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] *** This compares to year-end 2025 levels of $7.6 billion cash, $11.6 billion liquidity, and $2.1 billion net cash. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

Total debt is not explicitly labeled in the Q1 2026 earnings release, but can be inferred at approximately $5.6 billion ($8.8 billion cash less $3.2 billion net cash). The Q1 2026 10-Q indicates no scheduled minimum debt repayments for the remainder of 2026 or for 2027. [Source: Company Q1 2026 10-Q / Title: Q1 2026 10-Q / Date: 2026-04-23] *** As of June 30, 2025, scheduled maturities were $632 million in 2029 and $6.783 billion thereafter; however, debt was reduced by $3.4 billion during the second half of 2025, so this historical schedule is stale and should not be relied upon for current maturity profiling. [Source: Company Q2 2025 10-Q / Title: Q2 2025 10-Q / Date: 2025-07-24] *** [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

### Debt Maturity Profile & Refinancing Risk
With no mandatory maturities until at least 2028–2029, minimal refinancing risk, and a net cash position, the balance sheet is exceptionally strong. Moody's upgraded Newmont to A3 with a stable outlook in Q3 2025, citing the improved credit profile and liquidity. [Source: Company Q3 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Third Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2025-10-23] *** Interest expense is guided to approximately $175 million in 2026, net of capitalized interest. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

### Assessment
The balance sheet has swung from modest leverage (net debt of ~$5.35 billion at end-2024 per third-party estimates) to a net cash position in under 15 months, driven by asset sales and record operating cash flow. [Source: Monexa.ai / Title: Newmont Corporation (NEM): Cash Generation, Rapid Margin Recovery / Date: 2025-08-27] ** This provides substantial optionality for counter-cyclical investment or accelerated returns.

## 6. Capital Deployment & Returns on Capital

### Share Repurchases
Newmont has settled $6.0 billion of share repurchases under previously authorized programs through the Q1 2026 filing date, including $2.4 billion since the prior earnings call. In Q1 2026, the Board authorized an additional $6.0 billion program. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] *** In FY2025, total share repurchases settled were $2.3 billion, and in 2024 they were $1.25 billion. [Source: Company FY2025 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-19] *** [Source: Monexa.ai / Title: Newmont Corporation (NEM): Cash Generation, Rapid Margin Recovery / Date: 2025-08-27] ** The pace accelerated meaningfully in early 2026.

### Dividends
Newmont declared a Q1 2026 dividend of $0.26 per share, equivalent to an indicated annualized rate of $1.04 per share. Under the enhanced capital allocation framework announced in February 2026, the company targets a sustainable $1.1 billion annual cash dividend that grows on a per-share basis as repurchases reduce the share count. FY2025 total dividends paid were $1.01 per share. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] *** [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

### M&A / Capex
- **Development capital:** Guided to $1.4 billion in 2026, advancing Tanami Expansion 2, Cadia Panel Caves, Red Chris Block Cave, Cerro Negro Expansion, and Lihir Nearshore Barrier. Q1 2026 development spend was $239 million. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] ***
- **Sustaining capital:** Expected at approximately $1.95 billion in 2026, including critical tailings and infrastructure work at Cadia and Boddington. [Source: Kavout / Title: Is Newmont's New Capital Allocation Framework a Game Changer / Date: 2026-05-24] *
- **Exploration & advanced projects:** Guided to $525 million in 2026. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Divestitures:** The non-core divestiture program is complete, generating over $4.6 billion in total after-tax proceeds. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] ***

### Returns on Capital
Explicit ROIC or ROCE figures are not disclosed. However, the spread between realized gold prices and AISC provides a proxy for incremental capital returns. With Q1 2026 AISC at $1,029/oz against a $4,900/oz realized price, incremental ounces generate exceptionally high returns on invested capital. The 2026 guidance assumes this compresses to a still-healthy spread ($4,500 price assumption vs. $1,680 AISC guidance = $2,820/oz margin). Whether development projects earn above the cost of capital depends on execution; management emphasizes "highest-return free cash flow generative near-term projects." [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] ***

## 7. Management Guidance & Forward Outlook

### Most Recent Full Year Report Guidance
On February 19, 2026, Newmont provided 2026 guidance:
- Attributable gold production: ~5.3 million ounces (+/-5%)
- Copper: 102 thousand tonnes; Silver: 32 million ounces; Lead: 90 kt; Zinc: 220 kt
- Gold by-product CAS: $1,055/oz
- Gold by-product AISC: $1,680/oz
- Development capital: $1.4 billion
- Exploration & advanced projects: $525 million
- General & administrative: $375 million
- Interest expense: $175 million (net of capitalized interest)
- Depreciation & amortization: $2,815 million
- Reclamation and remediation accretion: $385 million
- Adjusted effective tax rate: 33%

Guidance is based on metal price assumptions of $4,500/oz gold, $5.00/lb copper, $60/oz silver, $0.90/lb lead, and $1.30/lb zinc, and FX rates of AUD/USD 0.70, CAD/USD 0.75, and USD/MXN 17.00. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***

### Most Recent Report Guidance
In the Q1 2026 earnings release (April 23, 2026), management reiterated full-year 2026 production guidance of 5.3 million attributable gold ounces and noted Q2 2026 would be slightly lower than Q1 due to temporary mill feed gaps. Full-year 2026 cost and capital guidance was unchanged. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: Newmont Corporation Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary / Date: 2026-04-24] *

### Management Commentary
Management emphasized a "disciplined focus on capital efficiency and value creation," highlighting the enhanced capital allocation framework designed to deliver predictable per-share dividend growth while maintaining a $1 billion net cash target (with +/- $2 billion flexibility). During strong commodity environments, the company intends to actively manage gross debt while maintaining a minimum $5 billion cash balance through the cycle. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] ***

### Forward Claim Confidence Weighting
- **2026 production of 5.3M oz:** Medium. Achievable but exposed to weather, grade variability, and co-product throughput (notably Peñasquito and Boddington).
- **2026 AISC of $1,680/oz:** Medium. Highly sensitive to silver/copper by-product credits, energy prices, and labor inflation. The $1,029/oz Q1 outcome is not repeatable at planned co-product prices and sustaining capital phasing.
- **Long-term ~6M oz gold + 150kt copper:** Low-Medium. Depends on Tanami Expansion 2 (H2 2027), Cadia panel caves, and Boddington stripping completion. Timeline slippage is common in mining.
- **Adjusted tax rate 33%:** High. Based on jurisdictional profit mix and explicit guidance.
- **Capital returns framework ($1.1B dividend baseline + buybacks):** High. This is a Board-approved policy with demonstrated execution; the only risk is a sustained collapse in gold prices.

## 8. Material Financial Risks & One-Off Items

- **Yanacocha Sulfides deferral and impairment:** In Q4 2025, Newmont recognized impairment charges of $779 million related to the decision to indefinitely defer the Yanacocha Sulfides project. Reserves were reclassified to resources. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Yanacocha reclamation liability:** Q4 2025 saw a net $137 million reduction in the total reclamation liability due to updated cost estimates, but cash spend on water treatment plants continues ($247 million in Q3 2025 alone). Peru political and community risks remain material. [Source: Company Q3 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Third Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2025-10-23] *** [Source: Company Q4 2025 10-K / Title: Q4 2025 10-K / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Q4 2025 tax charges:** The company recognized additional income taxes of $384 million for undistributed Papua New Guinea earnings and $165 million for Ghana earnings, reflecting a determination that excess cash could not be remitted on a tax-free basis. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Divestiture program completion:** Gains/losses on divestitures have created volatility. FY2025 benefited from net gains compared to prior-year write-downs, but the program is now complete, removing future divestiture proceeds. [Source: Company FY2025 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Operational disruptions:** Boddington bushfire and Tanami record rainfall in Q1 2026 demonstrate weather-related operational risk. Peñasquito has faced temporary suspensions in the past (Q1 2025 safety events). [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] *** [Source: Company Q3 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Third Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2025-10-23] ***
- **Co-product credit volatility:** By-product AISC is heavily influenced by copper and silver prices. A downturn in these metals would raise effective gold costs materially.

## 10. Key Financial Assumptions Embedded in Current Valuation

As of May 24, 2026, Newmont traded at approximately $107.60 per share, with a market capitalization near $114.9 billion and a forward P/E of roughly 10.4x. [Source: Robinhood / Title: NEM Stock Price Quote & News / Date: 2026-05-24] * [Source: StockAnalysis / Title: Newmont (NEM) Statistics & Valuation / Date: 2026-05-19] **

The current valuation appears to embed the following assumptions:
- **Sustained elevated gold prices:** The 2026 guidance assumes $4,500/oz, but spot prices near $5,000/oz and Q1 realized prices of $4,900/oz suggest the market is pricing in a prolonged high-price environment. Any reversion toward $3,000/oz or below would severely compress EBITDA and FCF.
- **Manageable cost inflation:** The market accepts the $1,680/oz AISC guidance for 2026 and assumes costs do not spiral further, despite labor and energy inflation across the sector.
- **Return to volume growth:** The stock likely prices in a return toward the ~6.0 million ounce longer-term production profile by 2027–2028, implying successful execution of Tanami Expansion 2, Boddington stripping, and Cadia/Lihir projects.
- **Continued aggressive capital returns:** The additional $6 billion buyback authorization and the per-share dividend growth framework are treated as durable policy rather than cyclical excess.
- **Stable fiscal regimes:** The valuation does not appear to price in severe royalty/tax increases in Ghana, Peru, or Papua New Guinea, nor does it price in prolonged operational suspensions at major assets.
- **Copper/silver price support:** By-product AISC calculations assume continued strong co-product metal prices. A collapse in copper or silver would raise effective gold costs and lower revenue simultaneously.

If these assumptions hold, the forward EV/EBITDA of ~6.6x looks reasonable for a dominant miner generating massive FCF. If gold prices mean-revert or costs overrun, the multiple expands quickly on lower earnings, exposing downside.

## 11. Bottom-Line Financial Assessment

### Business Model Characterization
Newmont is a Tier-1, globally diversified gold producer with meaningful copper, silver, zinc, and lead by-product exposure. Its financial reality is dictated by long-dated, capital-intensive asset bases, high operating leverage to metal prices, and the ongoing need to replace depleted reserves through exploration, development, or acquisition. The most meaningful financial lenses turned out to be: (1) the realized price-to-AISC margin per ounce; (2) free cash flow generation through the commodity cycle; and (3) reserve life and capital efficiency of the project pipeline. Standard retail metrics like gross margin are less informative than all-in sustaining margins and FCF conversion.

### Strengths (confidence weighted)
- **Balance sheet strength and liquidity (High):** Net cash of $3.2 billion, $12.8 billion total liquidity, no near-term debt maturities, and an A3 credit rating provide unmatched financial flexibility. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] *** [Source: Company Q3 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Third Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2025-10-23] ***
- **Record cash generation in elevated price environment (High):** FY2025 FCF of $7.3 billion and Q1 2026 FCF of $3.1 billion demonstrate the portfolio’s cash-conversion power when gold exceeds $4,500/oz. [Source: Company FY2025 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-19] *** [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] ***
- **Industry-leading reserve base (High):** 118.2 million attributable gold ounces and 12.5 million tonnes of copper reserves, calculated at a conservative $2,000/oz gold price, underpin decades of production. [Source: Company reserves press release / Title: Newmont Reports 2025 Mineral Reserves / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Cost competitiveness vs. major peer (Medium-High):** Guided 2026 AISC of $1,680/oz sits materially below Barrick’s guided $1,760–$1,950/oz range, suggesting relative margin protection. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] *** [Source: TradingView / Title: Barrick Mining vs. Newmont: Which Gold Giant Is Shining Brighter? / Date: 2026-04-20] *

### Weaknesses (confidence weighted)
- **Declining near-term production volume (High):** Attributable gold production fell 14% in 2025 and is guided down another ~10% in 2026 to 5.3 million ounces. This is a shrinking top-line volume profile despite price tailwinds. [Source: Company FY2025 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-19] *** [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Rising unit cost trajectory (High):** AISC is guided to rise from $1,358/oz in 2025 to $1,680/oz in 2026, driven by lower volumes, higher royalties linked to higher gold prices, and deferred sustaining capital. Cost inflation is industry-wide but erodes margin if metal prices stall. [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Jurisdictional and operational risk concentration (Medium):** Yanacocha (Peru) faces political, community, and environmental headwinds, resulting in the sulfides deferral and ongoing reclamation cash drains. Lihir (PNG) and Ghana also carry fiscal and geopolitical risk. [Source: Company Q4 2025 10-K / Title: Q4 2025 10-K / Date: 2026-02-19] *** [Source: Company Q4 2025 earnings release / Title: Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-19] ***
- **Dependence on co-product credits for cost metrics (Medium):** By-product AISC is highly sensitive to copper and silver prices. In a weaker base-metals environment, effective gold costs rise automatically. [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings release / Title: Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results / Date: 2026-04-23] ***

### Watch Items
- **Yanacocha reclamation and permitting cash flows:** Water treatment construction is consuming cash and could drive further liability adjustments.
- **Boddington stripping campaign completion:** Critical to unlocking higher gold/copper grades from 2027 onward.
- **Tanami Expansion 2 timeline:** Scheduled for H2 2027; delays would push back the return to 6M oz production.
- **Share count reduction pace:** With $6 billion in fresh buyback authorization and shares outstanding down ~3.8% year-over-year, the EPS uplift is material but depends on continued execution at prevailing prices.
- **Gold price and co-product metal price sensitivity:** The entire 2026 margin framework assumes elevated silver and copper prices. A synchronized pullback in all metals would compress both revenue and by-product credits.

## 12. Data Gaps & Limitations

- **FY2025 total revenue/sales figure:** No explicit full-year consolidated sales total was extracted from the 10-K or Q4 2025 earnings release tables. Search scope: FY2025 10-K highlights, Q4 2025 earnings release, Q1 2026 10-Q comparative tables. Analytical impact: exact revenue growth rate and EBITDA margin calculation cannot be verified from primary sources; approximate figures from third-party screens were avoided.
- **FY2024 full-year AISC:** The 2024 annual AISC figure of ~$1,516/oz was sourced only from financial press (Yahoo Finance) and not directly from a primary filing or earnings release. Search scope: 2024 Annual Report, Q4 2024 earnings release, FY2025 10-K comparative tables. Analytical impact: year-over-year cost comparison precision is reduced.
- **Complete Q1 2026 debt maturity ladder beyond 2027:** The Q1 2026 10-Q snippet confirmed no maturities in 2026 or 2027 but did not display the full schedule. Search scope: Q1 2026 10-Q Note 15. Analytical impact: precise refinancing risk profiling beyond 2027 is unavailable; reliance on stale Q2 2025 data is insufficient.
- **Q2 2026 results:** As of May 25, 2026, Q2 results have not been reported (Q1 reported April 23). Search scope: company investor relations, SEC filings. Analytical impact: no interim update on production, costs, or buyback execution since Q1.
- **Detailed segment-level revenue and profit breakdown for FY2025:** The 10-K segment tables were not fully extracted. Search scope: FY2025 10-K Part II Item 7. Analytical impact: geographic profit contribution and regional margin analysis is limited to cost guidance tables and qualitative commentary.
- **Peer financials for direct margin comparison:** Barrick and Agnico Eagle 2026 guidance was sourced from financial press, but detailed primary filings for peers were not reviewed. Search scope: Barrick Q4 2025 earnings, Agnico Eagle disclosures. Analytical impact: peer-relative positioning is based on management-guided figures rather than realized results.
- **Working capital component detail beyond Q3 2025 and Q1 2026:** Full-year 2025 working capital movements and Q4 2025 specifics were not separately detailed in the extracted sources. Analytical impact: cash conversion cycle analysis is incomplete.

# News & Narrative Deep Research: Newmont Corporation (NEM)

Research conducted: 2026-05-25

## 1. Most Significant News in the Window

**Q1 2026 Earnings Beat and Record Capital Returns (Event: Quarter ended March 31, 2026; Reported: April 23, 2026)**  
The dominant market-moving event in the window was Newmont’s first-quarter 2026 earnings release, which posted all-time quarterly records for free cash flow ($3.1 billion), adjusted EBITDA ($5.2 billion), and adjusted EPS ($2.90), crushing consensus estimates that had centered near $2.24 per share. Revenue of $7.31 billion also beat expectations by roughly $400–$740 million, driven by an average realized gold price of $4,900 per ounce and by-product AISC of just $1,029/oz—well below full-year guidance [Source: Company press release / Title: "Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow, Reports First Quarter 2026 Results" / Date: 2026-04-23]***. The board approved a fresh $6.0 billion share repurchase authorization after fully exhausting the prior $6.0 billion program, and declared a $0.26 quarterly dividend [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-23]***.

Despite the headline beat, the narrative reaction was bifurcated. The stock rallied sharply in the regular session on April 24 (+5.9% to +8.4% depending on measure) but remains approximately 20% below its January 2026 peak of $134.88, closing near $108 as of May 22 [Source: CNBC / Title: NEM Stock Price Quote / Date: 2026-05-22]**. This disconnect—record financials against a deteriorating stock chart—frames the current market debate.

**2026 Guidance Reset: The "Trough Year" Framing (Event: February 19, 2026)**  
Although released just outside the strict 90-day window, the February 19 guidance for 2026 remains the load-bearing narrative anchor. Management guided attributable gold production to ~5.3 million ounces (down ~10% from 2025) and by-product AISC to $1,680/oz, a sharp step-up from prior-year levels. This guidance introduced the "trough year" framing that has colored every subsequent earnings event [Source: Company press release / Title: "Newmont Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results, Provides 2026 Guidance" / Date: 2026-02-19]***.

**Nevada Gold Mines Notice of Default vs. Barrick (Event: February 3, 2026; Disclosed: February 20, 2026)**  
Newmont issued a formal notice of default to Barrick Gold alleging "systematic mismanagement" and resource diversion within the Nevada Gold Mines (NGM) joint venture, specifically claiming Barrick prioritized its 100%-owned Fourmile project at the expense of JV assets. The notice remains unresolved and was a central focus of the Q1 earnings Q&A [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: "Newmont Sends Notice of Default to Barrick Over Nevada Joint Venture" / Date: 2026-02-20]**. On the Q1 call, interim CFO Peter Wexler described the process as "open-ended" and "iterative," stating there is "no set timeline for bringing it to resolution" [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: "Newmont (NEM) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-23]*.

**Cadia Seismic Event (Event: April 15, 2026)**  
A 4.5-magnitude earthquake near the Cadia mine in Australia forced an underground suspension, temporarily knocking out one of Newmont’s largest gold-copper operations. Management guided to 80% capacity recovery within five weeks and full normalization by end of Q2, with Q2 production expected to be "slightly below" Q1 as a result [Source: Seeking Alpha / Title: "Newmont expects Cadia to return to 80% capacity in 5 weeks" / Date: 2026-04-23]*. The timing—days before earnings—amplified near-term cost and production anxiety.

**National Bank Downgrade on Cost Fears (Event: April 17, 2026)**  
National Bank Financial downgraded Newmont to Sector Perform from Outperform and cut its price target to $130 from $140, citing diesel inflation, Ghana’s new sliding-scale royalty, and operational disruptions at Cadia and Boddington. The downgrade explicitly framed the risk that cost headwinds could overwhelm record gold prices [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: "Newmont Downgraded as Costs Surge" / Date: 2026-04-17]*.

## 2. Earnings Call Deep Dive

### Headline Takeaways  
Management delivered a quarter of exceptional financial metrics but deliberately lowered near-term expectations. The prepared remarks emphasized that Q1’s $1,029/oz by-product AISC was artificially low due to favorable co-product pricing, timing of sustaining capital, and inventory builds. They repeatedly flagged that Q2 would be "notably higher" on costs and "slightly lower" on production [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: "Newmont (NEM) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-23]*.

### Management Tone & Conviction  
CEO Natascha Viljoen struck a disciplined, operational tone, opening with: "Newmont delivered strong operational and financial performance in the first quarter, producing approximately 1.3 million attributable gold ounces and generating an all-time record $3.1 billion in quarterly free cash flow, keeping us well on track to achieve our 2026 guidance" [Source: Company press release / Title: "Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow" / Date: 2026-04-23]***. On cost insulation, she noted the company is "actively working to mitigate the impact" of higher oil prices rather than viewing it as a risk to the operating plan [Source: Sahm Capital / Title: "Newmont Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-25]*.

Interim CFO Peter Wexler was more measured on the cost trajectory. He quantified the oil sensitivity ($12/oz AISC impact per $10/bbl Brent move) and acknowledged the Ghana royalty as a "$25 per ounce" incremental headwind, but maintained that "we view this as manageable at this time" [Source: Sahm Capital / Title: "Newmont Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-25]*. His language on the NGM dispute—"open-ended," "iterative," "no set timeline"—suggested management is either unwilling or unable to telegraph a near-term resolution, which appeared to frustrate analysts seeking closure.

### Q&A Pushback & Analyst Skepticism  
Two themes dominated analyst pushback:

**1. Cost durability and input inflation.** Lawson Winder of BofA Securities pressed directly on "the levers that Newmont Corporation can pull in order to ensure that input cost inflation does not drive 2026 unit cost guidance above the range." Wexler responded with macro sensitivities but offered no specific new cost-cut targets beyond "continued cost and productivity improvements" [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: "Newmont Corporation (NEM) Q1 FY2026 earnings call transcript" / Date: 2026-04-23]*. The exchange left the impression that management is relying on portfolio mix and co-product credits to offset macro inflation rather than operational restructuring.

**2. Nevada Gold Mines deadlock.** Both Tanya Jakusconek (Scotiabank) and Bob Brackett (Bernstein) pressed on the Barrick notice of default. Jakusconek sought a timeline; Wexler deflected. Brackett pushed on the "range of remedies" available to Newmont, including buy-sell provisions. The transcript shows Viljoen immediately handing the question to Wexler, who again emphasized process over outcome [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: "Newmont (NEM) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-23]*. The absence of any concrete remedy or timeline suggests the dispute is likely to be a multi-quarter overhang.

### Forward Commentary & Guidance  
Management maintained full-year 2026 production guidance of 5.3 million ounces and by-product AISC of $1,680/oz. They explicitly guided Q2 as the operational trough, with Cadia recovery and sustaining capital ramp-ups weighted to the second half. Development capital of $1.4 billion is also back-half weighted, with Red Chris feasibility work and the Lihir nearshore barrier project progressing [Source: Company Q1 2026 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-23]***.

## 3. Analyst Coverage & Rating Changes

### Consensus Picture  
As of late May 2026, the sell-side consensus skews bullish but with a wide dispersion that reflects uncertainty around gold price sustainability and cost execution. Among 24 Wall Street analysts, the average 12-month price target is approximately $142–$147, with a range of $107 (low) to $176 (high). The rating distribution sits at roughly 17 Buy/Overweight, 5 Hold, and 2 Strong Buy, yielding a "Moderate Buy" consensus [Source: MarketBeat / Title: "Newmont (NEM) Stock Forecast and Price Target 2026" / Date: 2026-05-24]* [Source: MarketWatch / Title: "Newmont Corp. Analyst Estimates" / Date: 2026-05-22]*. Most recent as of 2026-05-25.

### Specific Rating Actions in the Window  
- **National Bank Financial (Downgrade, April 17, 2026):** Cut to Sector Perform from Outperform; price target lowered to $130 from $140. Rationale: mounting cost pressures from diesel inflation, Ghana royalty regime, and Cadia/Boddington operational disruptions [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: "Newmont Downgraded as Costs Surge" / Date: 2026-04-17]*.  
- **BMO Capital Markets (Reiteration/Raise, April 24, 2026):** Maintained Outperform and raised price target to $145 from $140 following the Q1 beat and buyback expansion [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: NEM Analyst Recommendations / Date: 2026-05-23]*.  
- **Bernstein (Upgrade, February 27, 2026):** Upgraded to Outperform from Market-Perform and lifted price target to $157 from $121, reflecting a sharply higher long-term gold price deck ($4,800/oz for 2026, $6,100/oz by 2030) [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: "Bernstein upgrades Newmont on higher gold price forecasts" / Date: 2026-02-27]*.

The divergence between National Bank’s cost-focused downgrade and Bernstein’s gold-bull upgrade captures the current analyst debate: is Newmont a cost-driven value trap near-term, or a leverage play on structurally higher gold prices long-term?

## 4. Regulatory, Legal & Compliance Developments

**Nevada Gold Mines Contractual Dispute (Ongoing)**  
While not a government regulatory action, the notice of default against Barrick carries legal and operational overtones that could restructure the world’s largest gold-producing complex. Newmont alleges Barrick breached fiduciary duties by diverting shared JV resources to the adjacent Fourmile project. Barrick has publicly characterized the notice as a "tactical distraction" [Source: Financial Content / Title: "The Golden Divorce: Newmont Accuses Barrick of 'Resource Piracy'" / Date: 2026-03-31]*. No court filings or arbitration milestones have been announced since the February notice.

**Lihir Environmental Enforcement (Papua New Guinea)**  
On March 16, 2026, Papua New Guinea’s Conservation and Environmental Protection Authority (CEPA) issued an Enforcement Notice to Lihir Gold Limited (100% Newmont-owned) imposing new hazardous waste oil storage limits and an immaterial administrative penalty. Newmont stated in its 10-Q filing that compliance "would impact Lihir's operations" and is pursuing administrative remedies on the grounds that the new requirements conflict with the existing environmental permit [Source: SEC filing / Title: Newmont Q1 2026 10-Q / Date: 2026-04-23]***.

**Ghana Royalty and State Purchase Regime**  
Ghana’s newly introduced sliding-scale royalty is expected to add ~$25/oz to Newmont’s 2026 AISC. Separately, in May 2026, Reuters reported that Ghana seeks to purchase 30% of gold output from miners to bolster central bank reserves under the Ghana Gold Board framework—a policy shift that introduces potential offtake and repatriation risks [Source: Reuters / Title: "Ghana seeks to buy 30% of gold from miners to boost reserves" / Date: 2026-05-18]**.

**Australian Class Action**  
On March 17, 2026, a community class action was filed in the NSW Supreme Court against Newmont relating to dust emissions and groundwater impacts from the Cadia mine. The suit follows a $350,000 NSW EPA fine for air pollution violations in March 2025 [Source: Discovery Alert / Title: "Class Action Against Newmont: Mining Environmental Law" / Date: 2026-03-17]. No tag; use cautiously. This matter has not been cited as a near-term financial driver in mainstream financial coverage.

## 5. Corporate Events: M&A, Leadership, Restructuring, Capital Actions

### M&A Activity  
No material M&A announcements in the window. Management reiterated on the Q1 call that capital allocation prioritizes internal brownfield projects and that "any acquisitions required to compete for capital in the broader portfolio" [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: "Newmont (NEM) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-23]*. The non-core divestiture program has largely concluded, with $4.6 billion in after-tax proceeds raised since inception [Source: Company press release / Title: "Newmont Generates Record Quarterly Earnings and Free Cash Flow" / Date: 2026-04-23]***.

### Leadership Changes  
CEO Natascha Viljoen continues in her role, having taken the helm in advance of the 2026 guidance cycle. On the Q1 call, she confirmed the CFO search is ongoing, stating: "That recruitment process is going well, Matt, and we trust that we will be able to share more information soon" [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: "Newmont (NEM) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-23]*. Peter Wexler serves as interim CFO and Chief Legal Officer.

### Restructuring & Workforce Actions  
No material restructuring announcements in the window. The company has emphasized "productivity improvements" and organizational streamlining to fit the post-Newcrest integration core portfolio of 11 assets.

### Capital Return Announcements  
Newmont returned $2.7 billion to shareholders in Q1 via dividends and buybacks, exhausting the prior $6.0 billion authorization. The board immediately approved a new $6.0 billion program. Wexler noted on the call that "on a per share basis, our free cash flow is already 6% higher than it would have been prior to initiating our share repurchase program" [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: "Newmont (NEM) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-23]*. The market has generally read these actions as confidence signals, though some commentators frame aggressive buybacks as a substitute for growth given the 2026 production trough [Source: 24/7 Wall St / Title: "Newmont Downgraded as Costs Surge" / Date: 2026-04-17]*.

### Material Insider Transactions  
No open-market purchases by insiders were identified in the trailing six months per aggregator data; multiple sales were recorded, including by former CEO Thomas Palmer and other executives [Source: Quiver Quant / Title: "NEWMONT GOLD ($NEM) Releases Q1 2026 Earnings" / Date: 2026-04-23]. However, primary SEC Form 4 filings for the most recent quarter were not independently retrieved; this data should be treated as indicative rather than definitive. No unusual cluster buying or CEO open-market purchases were flagged.

## 6. Sentiment & Positioning

### Institutional Sentiment  
Aggregate 13F data as of late May 2026 shows institutional ownership at roughly 68.9% of shares outstanding, with 1,252 institutional buyers versus 758 sellers over the trailing 12 months, producing net inflows of approximately $13.7 billion against $8.7 billion in outflows [Source: MarketBeat / Title: "Newmont (NEM) Institutional Ownership 2026" / Date: 2026-05-26]*. Most recent as of 2026-05-25.  
Specific Q4 2025 13F moves (stale but directionally notable): UBS Asset Management reduced its position by ~75% (-24 million shares), while BlackRock increased by 7.6 million shares (+6.2%), VanEck added 5.6 million shares (+23.4%), and Arrowstreet Capital more than doubled its stake [Source: Quiver Quant / Title: "NEWMONT GOLD ($NEM) Releases Q1 2026 Earnings" / Date: 2026-04-23]. No tag; treat as lower-confidence aggregate.

### Retail & Social Sentiment  
Retail sentiment does not appear to be a material price driver for Newmont. The stock is not prominently featured in social media aggregators as a meme or retail momentum name. Any retail chatter tracks gold-price macro narratives rather than company-specific stories. Marked as lower-signal noise.

### Short Interest  
Short interest stood at 17.15 million shares as of April 30, 2026, representing 1.85% of float with a short ratio of 2.07 days to cover. This is down from 19.01 million shares in the prior period, suggesting short covering rather than accumulation [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: NEM Key Statistics / Date: 2026-05-23]* [Source: ShortSqueeze.com / Title: Short Squeeze Stock Short Interest for Newmont / Date: 2026-05-22]. Most recent as of 2026-05-25. The low short base indicates broad institutional comfort with the name, or at least a lack of dedicated bearish positioning.

### Valuation Narrative  
The market is actively debating whether Newmont deserves a premium multiple or is facing multiple compression. Following the Q1 beat, forward P/E sat near 10.2x (per CNBC data as of May 22), below the 52-week high implied multiple. The bull case argues that $4,500+ gold prices make even the $1,680 AISC guidance look cheap, while bears counter that 2026 is a production trough with rising cost curves and that the stock already discounted much of the gold rally during its run from $52 to $135 over the prior year [Source: Tikr / Title: "Newmont Stock Is Up 7% Amid Historic Gold Rally" / Date: 2026-02-17]*.

## 7. Short-Seller Research & Activist Investor Involvement

No short-seller reports or activist 13D filings were identified in the research window. Short interest remains below 2% of float, indicating no concentrated bearish campaign. No activist investor involvement identified in the research window.

## 8. Narrative Shifts

**Prior Narrative (Late 2025–January 2026):** Newmont as the premier leveraged play on a structural gold bull market, riding record prices, post-Newcrest synergy realization, and margin expansion. The stock doubled off its May 2025 lows and peaked at $134.88 on January 29, 2026.

**Current Narrative (May 2026):** Newmont as a "trough year" story where record gold prices are necessary to offset a 10% production decline, rising AISC, geopolitical cost headwinds (Ghana, oil), and an unresolved JV dispute with Barrick. The market has rotated from rewarding top-line gold leverage to punishing cost execution risk.

**Trigger:** The narrative shift was triggered by the February 19, 2026 guidance release, which framed 2026 as a lower-volume, higher-cost year. It was reinforced by the April 15 Cadia earthquake and the National Bank downgrade, which crystallized investor fears that Q1’s spectacular margins were not sustainable through the year. Even the Q1 earnings beat failed to reset the narrative because management explicitly guided Q2 as weaker.

## 9. Narrative-vs-Reality Disconnects

A material disconnect appears to exist between the operational/financial reality of Q1 and the stock’s forward-looking narrative. Newmont generated $3.1 billion in quarterly free cash flow, maintains a net cash position (~$3.2 billion), and is buying back stock aggressively, yet the equity trades ~20% below its January high and roughly in line with early-2026 levels. The market appears to be pricing in a mean reversion in gold prices toward $4,000/oz and/or a breach of the $1,680 AISC guidance ceiling.

If gold prices sustain near $4,500–$5,000 and Q2 costs peak as guided, the 2026 free cash flow generation could end up well above what the current stock price implies. Conversely, if gold corrects below $4,200, the bear narrative is validated. The disconnect is therefore conditional on gold-price sustainability rather than purely operational.

## 10. Bottom-Line Narrative Assessment

### Narrative Characterization  
Newmont is currently perceived as a high-quality but de-risking gold giant caught between a record macro environment and a micro operational trough. The market narrative is skeptical of near-term cost control and production recovery, treating Q1 as a cyclical high-water mark rather than a new baseline. The unresolved Barrick dispute and Ghana regulatory shifts add jurisdictional risk premiums that offset the gold-price premium.

### Key Narrative Drivers (confidence weighted)

1. **Gold price sustainability and central bank demand** — *High confidence*. The realized Q1 price of $4,900/oz and forward analyst decks (Bernstein $4,800; J.P. Morgan $5,055) are the primary drivers of revenue and FCF estimates. Widely held.  
2. **Q2 2026 as the cost/production trough** — *Medium confidence*. Management has clearly messaged this, but analyst skepticism persists given Cadia recovery timing and oil/royalty headwinds.  
3. **Capital return accretion from $6B buyback** — *Medium confidence*. The buyback is real and ongoing, but its narrative power is diluted by the production decline (i.e., returning cash because growth options are limited).  
4. **Nevada Gold Mines dispute resolution path** — *Low confidence*. No timeline, no disclosed remedies, and management language is non-committal.  
5. **Cost discipline / AISC containment at $1,680 guidance** — *Medium confidence*. Q1 was a beat, but Q2 is guided "notably higher." The full-year target is the line in the sand for analyst sentiment.

### Narrative Risks (confidence weighted)

1. **Gold price correction below $4,200/oz** — *High confidence*. The single biggest risk to the bull case. With AISC guided at $1,680, margin compression would be severe and rapid.  
2. **Cost inflation breaching 2026 guidance** — *Medium confidence*. Oil, Ghana royalties, and sustaining capital ramp-ups are known headwinds; if they compound, the February guidance is at risk.  
3. **NGM dispute escalation or buy-sell trigger** — *Low confidence*. A forced buy-sell or arbitration would be a multi-billion-dollar capital event and distract management, but probability and timing are opaque.  
4. **Q2 production miss or Cadia delayed recovery** — *Medium confidence*. Management expects normalization by Q3; any slippage would reinforce the "trough year" bear narrative.

### Catalysts to Watch

- **Q2 2026 earnings release:** Expected late July 2026 (consensus estimate July 23 or 29). This will test whether Q2 was indeed the trough or whether costs/production diverged further from guidance.  
- **Cadia operations update:** Full capacity recovery guided by end of Q2 2026 (June 30). Any deviation would be an immediate trading catalyst.  
- **Ex-dividend date:** May 27, 2026 ($0.26/share) [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: NEM Stock Quote / Date: 2026-05-23]*.  
- **NGM dispute developments:** No scheduled dates; monitor Barrick regulatory filings and Newmont quarterly updates for any change in tone or legal escalation.  
- **Red Chris feasibility/FID:** Management indicated intention to reach FID in H2 2026. Updated capital estimate expected ahead of that decision [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: "Newmont (NEM) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-23]*.  
- **Ghana Gold Board implementation:** Formal agreements with large-scale miners expected mid-May 2026; watch for impact on offtake terms or operational restrictions [Source: Mining Technology / Title: "Ghana expands gold purchase programme" / Date: 2025-05-01]**. Note: This source is from 2025 and may reflect historical program details rather than 2026 updates.

## 11. Data Gaps & Limitations

- **Options positioning and flow data:** No reliable options market positioning data found; search scope: Yahoo Finance options chain, MarketBeat options data, general web queries for NEM options activity. Impact: Limits ability to assess near-term directional sentiment or hedging pressure.  
- **Q1 2026 specific 13F institutional changes:** Most recent granular institutional buy/sell data reflects Q4 2025 filings (reported through February/March 2026). Search scope: MarketBeat, Quiver Quant, WhaleWisdom queries. Impact: Cannot confirm whether the post-April earnings rally was accompanied by fresh institutional accumulation or distribution.  
- **Complete Q&A transcript for Bob Brackett’s follow-up on NGM remedies:** The transcript source cut off before Peter Wexler’s full response to the remedies question. Search scope: Motley Fool, Seeking Alpha, Yahoo Finance transcript pages. Impact: Limits precision on management’s legal stance, though the general non-committal tone is established.  
- **Immediate after-hours vs. next-day stock reaction clarity:** Sources conflicted on whether NEM dipped modestly after hours on April 23 or opened sharply higher on April 24. Search scope: Investing.com, CNBC, GuruFocus, Quiver Quant. Impact: Minor; the more important data point is the sustained inability to reclaim January highs.  
- **Verified recent insider Form 4 filings:** Aggregator data references trades attributed to executives, but independent SEC EDGAR Form 4 review was not completed. Search scope: Yahoo Finance insider page, Quiver Quant. Impact: Insider selling characterization should be treated as tentative.  
- **Retail/social sentiment metrics:** No material retail sentiment signal found; search scope: Reddit, StockTwits, Twitter volume queries. Impact: Confirms retail is not a driver, but no quantitative metric available.
